disposition: Shortage Loss – Common L1 checks (apply to S1–S5)

checks.description: Verify the Captain entered the correct AWB numbers; if not, request correct inputs.
Open the Valmo Lost Data query (Metabase) and check lost attribution vs the AWB ('Remarks' shows if attribution is pending).
Cross-validate using the Shortage SOP and Log10 detailed scans against the AWBs.
Check the Shortage Data Query to confirm evidence/reply mails were sent to both partners; if not, requeue to both / the respective hubs.

resolution.action: Check the Shortage Data Query to confirm evidence/reply mails were sent to both partners; if not, requeue to both / the respective hubs.

disposition: Shortage Loss – S1: Evidence/reply mails not sent to one or all partners

preconditions: Wrong loss is marked on me.

checks.description: Requeue the evidence mails to the partner(s) via escalation.

resolution.action: Direct L1→L3 escalation on Kapture (requeue evidence mails).

params.escalation_criteria: Direct L1→L3 escalation on Kapture (requeue evidence mails).

escalation.handover: neutral flow

doc_reference.l1_process:
1. L1 agent checks if AWB Numbers have been rightly added by the Captain. If not, L1 agent requests Captains for the right inputs.
2. L1 agent refers to this Metabase query to check the lost attribution against the AWB. The “Remarks” column in the query tells agents on whether lost attribution is pending or not.
3. For c ross-validation, L1 agent will refer to the Shortage SOP. Additionally, L1 agents will also refer to Log 10 to check the detailed scans against the AWBs.
4. L1 agent will check the Shortage Data Query to re-verify if the mails are sent to the partner. If they are not sent to BOTH partners, the mail needs to be triggered again to both or the respective hubs.
5. Basis steps 2-3 and the scenarios below, the L1 agent will abide by the following:

Scenario 1: The Evidence and reply mails have not been sent to one or all partners - The mails need to be requeued to the partners (through escalation).
- This is a direct L1 to L3 escalation on kapture.
doc_reference.l2_l3_process:
L2 and L3 agents come into play when the tickets are escalated. L2 agents must validate the escalation remarks to verify if the escalation made is valid.In case of escalation in the following scenarios, the L2 agents should follow these SOPs 1. L1 agent marks the ticket for reversal: L2 agent must check the reply and remarks added by the L1 agent and forward the corresponding shipments to the wrong losses sheet, filling in the appropriate columns – shared with the losses functional team.
2. The L1 agent is unable to access the information needed to resolve the ticket; the L2 agent must reverify it. If the information is available, then the agent must offer the best possible resolution according to the SOPs. If the information is not available, then the ticket must be worked out along with the functional teams.

checks.description: Verify the Captain entered the correct AWB numbers; if not, request correct inputs.
Open the Valmo Lost Data query (Metabase) and check lost attribution vs the AWB ('Remarks' shows if pending).
Cross-validate using the Hardstop SOP and Log10 detailed scans.
For scans at PCXX nodes (Packaging Centers), shipments were consolidated under a parent AWB — process the parent AWB (find it via Single AWB Tracking).

resolution.action: For scans at PCXX nodes (Packaging Centers), shipments were consolidated under a parent AWB — process the parent AWB (find it via Single AWB Tracking).

disposition: Hardstop – S1.1: Wrong loss; scans valid; Hardstop SOP followed

preconditions: Wrong loss is marked on me.

checks.description: Check Log10 scans + Hardstop SOP (forward connection within 5 days reverse / 7 days forward).
Raise for reversal to the respective L2.

resolution.action: Reversal escalation to respective L2.

doc_reference.l1_process:
1. L1 agent checks if AWB Numbers have been rightly added by the Captain. If not, L1 agent requests Captains for the right inputs.
2. L1 agent refers to this Metabase query to check the lost attribution against the AWB. The remarks column in the query tells agents on whether lost attribution is pending or not.
3. For cross-validation, L1 agent will refer to the Hardstop SOP. Additionally, L1 agent will also refer to Log10 to check the detailed scans against the AWBs.
4. While looking at the scans – scans that mention nodes with the naming PCXX are the general nomenclature for Packaging Centers – this means that the AWBs were consolidated into a parent package with a parent AWB. These AWBs must not be further processed, the agent must proceed with the parent AWB.
5. Parent AWB can be found by inputting the given AWB into this single AWB tracking with the AWB value as the filter.
6. Basis these scenarios, L1 agents will act in the following ways:

Scenario 1.1: Loss marked – Captains claim connection (Wrong loss marked) – Scans valid – Hardstop SOP followed - Agent checks the scan data on Log10 and also checks the Hardstop SOP
- Hardstop rule: any shipment arriving to the hub should be connected forward within 5 days(reverse) or 7 days(forward).
- Raises it for reversal to the respective L2.
doc_reference.guardrails:
Current Guardrail: Requests from AM on AWB level (Requested by agents to Captains in the ticket itself) current Guardrail: 1. Requests from AM OR 1. Prior requests from Captains due to Tech Issues / Ops Issues - We have shared a new requirement with Kapture to add an additional field prompting captains to share any tech/ops ticket ID they might have created with regard to the issue for Agents to input in the ticket.
- For Tech tickets, L1-L2 Agents need to be provided access to Log10 (To-Do) to check whether a prior tech ticket has been raised in this regard.
- For Ops tickets, once L1 agents can check the ticket in Kapture platform itself.OR 1. Captain informs beforehand that he received a damaged shipment and could not process the shipment - In the short term, L1 agents will be provided access to the damage shipment tracker (link). These trackers are refreshed on a monthly level. Monthly data to be shared with Support lead (Nandini Raj P S) who will share it with recommended L1-L2 agents
- In the long run, will try to have this within Kapture OR 1. Hardstop Loss can be waived off under the following circumstances - Tenurity of the captain: <60 days AND
- 0 hardstop reversals done in the past AND
- At least 1 payment cycle invoice generated AND
- Current hardstop pendency = 0

checks.description: Step 1: Identify the loss marked AWB* When the Captain raises a request stating that the FE has been marked with a wrong loss, the agent needs to first enter the **FE ID or Phone Number** in query:https://metabase-main.bi.meeshogcp.in/question/187207-qc-fail* Apply the **exact time range** when the loss was marked.* The Captain will share the **loss marked amount**.* The agent needs to locate the same amount on the mentioned date and identify the **AWB number** against which the loss was marked.

Step 2: Check the debit reason* Enter the identified **AWB number** in query:https://metabase-main.bi.meeshogcp.in/question/187207-qc-fail* Check the **debit_reason** column.* The possible debit reasons are:* LM Secondary QC junk image* Pickup junk image* ICUD junk image* Pickup vs QC image mismatch.

Step 3: Identify the node where loss was marked* If the debit reason is **LM Secondary QC junk image** → Loss is marked on the **LM Hub**.* If the debit reason is **Pickup junk image** → Loss is marked on the **LM FE (Pickup FE)**.* If the debit reason is **ICUD junk image** → Loss is marked on the **LM FE (ICUD FE - Forward leg)**.* If the debit reason is **Pickup vs QC image mismatch** → Loss is marked on the **LM FE**.

Step 4: Validate images and take action* The agent needs to review the images captured at the respective node where the loss was marked.* Check whether the entity has followed the required SOP.* If the entity has followed the SOP → The marked loss will be reversed.* If the entity has not followed the SOP → The loss will be considered valid and the entity will bear the loss.

resolution.action: Direct L2 escaltion if a image mismatch is ientified by L1 agent

params.l2_sop:
1. L2 agent must review the ticket and the loss marked.
2. If the loss is correctly marked then response to captain
3. If the loss is rightly contested then L2 agent must raise it for reversal on the dashboard made by the SX team.
4. SX team is to check the loss and confirm the re-attribution within 48 hours.
params.escalation_criteria: Direct L2 escaltion if a image mismatch is ientified by L1 agent

escalation.handover:
L2: 1. L2 agent must review the ticket and the loss marked.
2. If the loss is correctly marked then response to captain
3. If the loss is rightly contested then L2 agent must raise it for reversal on the dashboard made by the SX team.
4. SX team is to check the loss and confirm the re-attribution within 48 hours.

Scenario 1: RVP consumables payment not received - Payment received for X out of Y consumables (X<Y):
- This particular scenario is highly prone to fraud – Captain use 3P consumables, consumables provided to them in the forward leg or leftover consumables from previous orders to raise refunds for more consumables then they had ordered.
- This fraud can be mitigated by having validation set up to check which packets were ordered then vs everything that was raised for the refund.
- Agent must enter the hub ID in the captain payout query and check the mentioned payment cycles by the partners or follow the proactive steps.
- For the mentioned billing cycles the partner must check the columns rvp_consumable_amount, rvp_consumable_packet_count and rvp_consumable_rate. (rate x count = amount).

disposition: Hardstop Loss - Loss marked - Captain claims wrong loss on hub or FE – SX claims issue

preconditions: Wrong Loss is marked on me

checks.description: L1 agent checks if AWB Numbers have been rightly added by the Captain. If not, L1 agent requests Captains for the right inputs.
L1 agent refers to this Metabase query to check the lost attribution against the AWB. The remarks column in the query tells agents on whether lost attribution is pending or not.
For cross-validation, L1 agent will refer to the Hardstop SOP. Additionally, L1 agent will also refer to Log10 to check the detailed scans against the AWBs.

While looking at the scans – scans that mention nodes with the naming PCXX are the general nomenclature for Packaging Centers – this means that the AWBs were consolidated into a parent package with a parent AWB. These AWBs must not be further processed, the agent must proceed with the parent AWB.
Parent AWB can be found by inputting the given AWB into this single AWB tracking with the AWB value as the filter.
Basis these scenarios, L1 agents will act in the following ways:

Scenario 13: Loss marked - Captain claims wrong loss on hub or FE – SX claims issue:Step 1: Identify the loss marked AWB When the Captain raises a request stating that the FE has been marked with a wrong loss, the agent needs to first enter the FE ID or Phone Number in query:https://metabase-main.bi.meeshogcp.in/question/187207-qc-fail Apply the exact time range when the loss was marked. The Captain will share the loss marked amount. The agent needs to locate the same amount on the mentioned date and identify the AWB number against which the loss was markedStep 2: Check the debit reason Enter the identified AWB number in query:https://metabase-main.bi.meeshogcp.in/question/187207-qc-fail Check the debit_reason column. The possible debit reasons are: LM Secondary QC junk image Pickup junk image ICUD junk image Pickup vs QC image mismatch.Step 3: Identify the node where loss was marked If the debit reason is LM Secondary QC junk image → Loss is marked on the LM Hub. If the debit reason is Pickup junk image → Loss is marked on the LM FE (Pickup FE). If the debit reason is ICUD junk image → Loss is marked on the LM FE (ICUD FE - Forward leg). If the debit reason is Pickup vs QC image mismatch → Loss is marked on the LM FE.Step 4: Validate images and take action The agent needs to review the images captured at the respective node where the loss was marked. Check whether the entity has followed the required SOP. If the entity has followed the SOP → The marked loss will be reversed. If the entity has not followed the SOP → The loss will be considered valid and the entity will bear the loss.

Blacklisted
Temporarily Inactive
If the status shows Blacklisted, Escalate to L2.
If the status shows temporarily Inactive, check in the Autopay Whitelist L2 query, This query will show two reasons broadly:
Not - Whitelisted
Whitelisted
